jokes.text: Previously on 24... I mean The Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Self-aware correction acknowledging the 24 parody format
jokes.explanation: Clean meta joke that establishes the parody premise efficiently

jokes.text: Head Hall Monitor Loves Poneis / Hates Phonies
jokes.setup: Character identification card in serious counter-terrorism context
jokes.punchline: Childish misspelling of 'ponies' undermines the serious tone
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that undermines the parody's seriousness

jokes.text: Enhance! Skinner Stinks Dehance! Dehance!
jokes.punchline: Reveals childish graffiti, then 'dehancing' to hide embarrassing detail
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of the enhance trope with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: Principal / Mama's Boy
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest personal detail in professional context
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner character detail that's both accurate and embarrassing

jokes.text: We helped.
jokes.setup: Database gets praised for good work
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's pathetic attempt to share credit
jokes.explanation: Classic Milhouse - desperately trying to be included, perfectly delivered with minimal words

jokes.text: ATM User Devoted Father of Two
jokes.setup: Character identification for minor background character
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and irrelevant personal information
jokes.explanation: Amusing background detail that parodies overly detailed character information

jokes.text: you song-less land whale
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and creative insult combining two unrelated disparaging terms
jokes.explanation: Creative insult that's both harsh and weirdly poetic

jokes.text: I'll teach you to make a Poupon 'me.'
jokes.setup: Homer responding to Grey Poupon reference
jokes.punchline: Mispronouncing 'poop on' as fancy mustard brand
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism that's both crude and accidentally sophisticated

jokes.text: If someone did eat Bart's shorts, they'd have a tummy full of pocket garbage.
jokes.setup: Reference to classic Simpsons catchphrase 'Eat my shorts'
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of what would actually happen
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that takes a familiar phrase to its logical conclusion

jokes.text: Or maybe it's a.m. Whichever one is the morning one, it's not that one.
jokes.punchline: Narrator admits confusion about basic time concepts
jokes.explanation: Breaks the fourth wall by having narrator admit incompetence

jokes.text: If a dead fish and a homeless person had a baby, and the baby puked, and a dog ate the puke, this smells like the rear end of that dog!
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate and gross comparison that keeps building
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating comparison that gets more absurd with each step

jokes.text: Let's spit in the coffee pots. I call hazelnut.
jokes.punchline: Treating disgusting vandalism like choosing flavors normally
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing bullies' casual approach to gross behavior

jokes.text: Now this crossword's nothin' but swears.
jokes.punchline: Innocent observation about inappropriate content
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment that feels authentic to Willie

jokes.text: Permission to buy a Hostess fruit pie to keep my strength up? Denied.
jokes.punchline: Milhouse asking permission for snack food in serious tone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - treats serious situation childishly

jokes.text: Hey, lay off. You're the fat one of you guys! I'm bulking up for football!
jokes.punchline: Defensive response with obvious athletic excuse
jokes.explanation: Standard defensive response joke, nothing particularly clever

jokes.text: You call that playing to win?
jokes.punchline: Treating serious violence like a competitive sport
jokes.explanation: Shows bully mentality but not particularly original

jokes.text: This is our life now, Milhouse. We're Dumpster folk.
jokes.setup: Homer and Milhouse hiding in dumpster
jokes.punchline: Homer dramatically accepting their new identity as dumpster dwellers
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to dramatically accept absurd situations

jokes.text: He'll be somewhat missed.
jokes.punchline: Underwhelming emotional response to friend's disappearance
jokes.explanation: Perfectly deadpan response that undercuts the dramatic tension

jokes.text: Lord, give me guidance. That's right, the Guidance Department Detention Room. Thank you, Mrs. Lord.
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets his prayer as referring to school guidance counselor
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that perfectly fits Skinner's literal-minded character

jokes.text: Kids love the zing of raisins!
jokes.setup: Marge talking about her cake ingredients
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic endorsement of notoriously unpopular cake ingredient
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - earnestly promoting something kids hate

jokes.text: Wow, that's a swear? Used as a noun it is. Sweet!
jokes.setup: Bart learning a new swear word
jokes.punchline: Grammar lesson makes the word more appealing to Bart
jokes.explanation: Shows both characters - Skinner's pedantry and Bart's enthusiasm for bad words

jokes.text: six weeks of bottled farts
jokes.setup: List of disgusting stink bomb ingredients
jokes.punchline: Impossibly gross ingredient presented matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that's absurd but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Put a top on, Mom! Everyone's seen your implants. But the doctor said the air will help them... Just go!
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing overshare about medical procedure in casual context
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable family dynamic that's both realistic and cringe-worthy

jokes.text: Lookin' good, Carol!
jokes.punchline: Kid inappropriately complimenting friend's mother
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment that adds to the cringe factor

jokes.text: The teacher gave him a frowny-face.
jokes.setup: Discussing accuracy of Jimbo's house drawings
jokes.punchline: Academic critique using elementary school grading system
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing the elementary school context

jokes.text: Scram... boiled eggs would be lovely for breakfast, Mother.
jokes.punchline: Quickly switches to polite conversation when mom appears
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing tough kid instantly becoming polite with mom

jokes.text: Damn it, these blue prints are all wrong. Where's the roller coaster room and the shark tank?
jokes.punchline: Expecting absurdly elaborate rooms in ordinary suburban home
jokes.explanation: Bart's unrealistic expectations about what houses should contain

jokes.text: Son of a beach ball, they're gone!
jokes.punchline: Censored version of profanity using innocent beach item
jokes.explanation: Standard TV-friendly curse substitute, fits show's style

jokes.text: Ahmed Adoody. Does anyone there know Ahmed Adoody? Ahmed Adoody, wealthy Saudi financier. Really? No, Jack, it's a joke name.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate explanation of obviously fake scatological name
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call name that works well in the 24 parody context

jokes.text: 20 minutes at 300 degrees is the equivalent of five minutes at... 1,200 degrees.
jokes.setup: Marge trying to speed up baking process
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong understanding of how cooking temperatures work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge logic - well-intentioned but completely wrong

jokes.text: Pink frosting fixes everything.
jokes.setup: Marge has clearly ruined her cake somehow
jokes.punchline: Simple solution that obviously won't work
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Marge - naive optimism about cosmetic solutions to fundamental problems

jokes.text: Say, is your butt numb from all this vibrating? Oh, mine is. Still, I wouldn't describe it as unpleasant.
jokes.setup: Two characters hiding in vibrating dumpster
jokes.punchline: Homer making inappropriate observation to child about physical sensation
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable adult-child interaction that relies on innuendo

jokes.text: Oh, sweet mother of Mary!
jokes.setup: Milhouse experiencing pain from glass removal
jokes.punchline: Child using elaborate religious exclamation for minor medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Milhouse's tendency toward dramatic overreaction to minor things

jokes.text: Old Betsy'll get us there.
jokes.punchline: Willie's affectionate name for presumably beaten-up vehicle
jokes.explanation: Fits Willie's character but isn't particularly funny on its own

jokes.text: This is as boring as mainstream cinema.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious film criticism from elementary school student
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin pretension - art house snobbery from a kid

jokes.text: This is as boring as mainstream cinema.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious comparison to film criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin - turning a simple observation into intellectual snobbery

jokes.text: What is it about a woman in a Dumpster?
jokes.punchline: Treating bizarre situation as somehow romantically appealing
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Wiggum line that treats the bizarre as normal

jokes.text: They're gonna wish the bake sale was a nose plug sale.
jokes.setup: Bullies about to deploy stink bomb
jokes.punchline: Logical wordplay about people needing nose plugs
jokes.explanation: Functional joke but predictable - obvious setup and payoff

jokes.text: And it totally isn't.
jokes.setup: Commenting that bake sale should be nose plug sale
jokes.punchline: Obvious statement delivered as if it's insightful
jokes.explanation: Minor joke about stating the obvious as revelation

jokes.text: I'll mop up your blood from the inside!
jokes.punchline: Violent threat using cleaning metaphor and impossible logistics
jokes.explanation: Great Willie threat that's both violent and absurdly specific to his job

jokes.text: Skip, skip, skip to my Lou... Top of the morning, Nelson. And to you, Martin.
jokes.setup: Martin skipping and singing children's song
jokes.punchline: Nelson politely responding instead of bullying, which is completely out of character
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character subversion - Nelson being polite is immediately suspicious

jokes.text: Can't a guy enjoy a catchy tune?
jokes.setup: Nelson not bullying Martin as expected
jokes.punchline: Innocent explanation for out-of-character behavior
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Nelson acting out of character with reasonable explanation before the reveal

jokes.text: You're on a Bluetooth cell phone, the most vulnerable device known to man. But it looks so cool.
jokes.punchline: Lisa prioritizing appearance over security concerns
jokes.explanation: Good tech satire showing vanity overriding practical concerns

jokes.text: You're on a Bluetooth cell phone, the most vulnerable device known to man. But it looks so cool.
jokes.punchline: Lisa prioritizing appearance over safety
jokes.explanation: Good tech humor showing vanity trumping practicality - relevant character moment

jokes.text: Rice Krispie Treats?! Way to phone it in, Sarah Wiggum.
jokes.punchline: Harsh criticism of low-effort baked good with specific attribution
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary about competitive parenting at school events

jokes.text: All here, from Asimov to Zarathustra.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious alphabetical reference combining sci-fi author with philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin pretension with clever A-to-Z structure using intellectual references

jokes.text: Smell you later. Three minutes later, to be exact.
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation referencing stink bomb timing
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that ties common phrase to the specific plot

jokes.text: Don't worry, it's not too moist.
jokes.punchline: Promoting dryness as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - trying to spin obvious flaws as positive features with complete sincerity

jokes.text: All the other husbands don't come home dirty from fighting. I had to fight. Lenny said his dad is Hulk Hogan, and I know he's not.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he needs more money
jokes.punchline: Childish playground dispute about wrestling celebrity parentage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - adult getting into kids' arguments

jokes.text: Hey, I'm fine. They haven't made a French horn yet that can hurt me.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific boast about immunity to musical instrument damage
jokes.explanation: Absurd tough-guy boast that's both specific and pointless

jokes.text: That's a class-five stench-dispersal unit.
jokes.punchline: Technical military terminology for children's prank device
jokes.explanation: Good parody of technical jargon applied to juvenile prank

jokes.text: There's over 30 years of wiener soakings in there.
jokes.punchline: Disgusting description of accumulated food service liquid
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor about institutional food service

jokes.text: And I just sharpened those blades.
jokes.setup: Characters about to hit fan blades
jokes.punchline: Willie's pride in maintenance work despite impending violence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie line - maintenance pride trumps concern for human safety

jokes.text: As you're dying, jam the blades with your spine.
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting brother use his death as problem-solving tool
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark joke combining tactical thinking with sibling indifference

jokes.text: With seven cups of coffee still in it. Oh, wait, six.
jokes.punchline: Correcting himself after taking a sip during emergency
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priorities - coffee consumption during crisis

jokes.text: Oh, that's convenient.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging absurd plot convenience
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about contrived plot resolution

jokes.text: I don't want to live if I have to clean up this mess.
jokes.setup: Willie surveying the aftermath of the crisis
jokes.punchline: Preferring death to janitorial work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie - his job frustration so extreme he'd rather die than do extra cleaning

jokes.text: Oh, I'm just glad you taste like hot dogs.
jokes.punchline: Motherly affection expressed through reference to hot dog water contamination
jokes.explanation: Sweet Marge moment that references earlier plot point

jokes.text: I pulled every single field agent off all other cases to track you down and bring you to justice.
jokes.setup: Jack Bauer explaining his response to prank call
jokes.punchline: Massively disproportionate law enforcement response to minor prank
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke about authority figure overreacting

jokes.text: It's okay, that was Shelbyville.
jokes.setup: Concern about pulling agents off other cases
jokes.punchline: Dismissing consequences because it affects rival town
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Simpsons geography with dark implications
